stories.title: Byron Nelson R1 Recap — Moore Leads at 62, Koepka 63, Scheffler 66 at TPC Craig Ranch
stories.angle: Moore's 62 is impressive, but Koepka's story — bogey-free 63 on the strength of a new putter, his best round of 2026 — is the headline grabber. Scheffler lurks at -5 in a course he won last year.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Byron Nelson R1 final:

Taylor Moore: -9 (62) — leader
Brooks Koepka: -8 (63) — bogey-free, new putter
Si Woo Kim: -7 (64)
Scottie Scheffler: -5 (66) — 4 back, lurking

R2 underway today. Golf Channel 3-7 PM ET. #GolfFanrecap #ByronNelson #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
LIVE: Byron Nelson R2. Scheffler/Koepka/Kim teed off ~1:43 PM ET.

Scheffler is 4 back. He's defending. He's dangerous.

Watch on Golf Channel. Can Moore hold the lead? #GolfFanrecap #ByronNelson #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Round 1 of the CJ Cup Byron Nelson at TPC Craig Ranch told a familiar and exciting story: a surprise leader, a dominant champion lurking, and a suddenly dangerous former major winner rediscovering his form.

Taylor Moore shot a 9-under 62 to take the first-round lead — a round that set the tone for a packed leaderboard just behind him. Brooks Koepka, deploying a new putter for the first time, carded a bogey-free 8-under 63 that was the talk of the clubhouse Thursday. Si Woo Kim sits at 7-under, and then — four back but absolutely in striking distance — is Scottie Scheffler.

Scheffler won this tournament last year. He is the world No. 1. He is playing at a course he knows how to win on. A 66 in Round 1 is exactly the kind of position that sets up a controlled, patient charge over the next three days.

Round 2 is underway today at TPC Craig Ranch, with Koepka, Scheffler, and Kim in the same group teeing off in the afternoon. Golf Channel has coverage from 3-7 PM ET. This leaderboard is tight and the weekend will be compelling.
facebook.image_caption:
Byron Nelson R1: Moore 62 leads. Koepka 63 one back. Scheffler 66, four back.

The defending champion is lurking. Round 2 underway now.

Golf Channel 3-7 PM ET. #GolfFanrecap #ByronNelson #PGATour

article.headline: Byron Nelson Round 1: Moore's 62 Leads, Koepka Fires 63, Scheffler Lurks Four Back

stories.title: Koepka's Putter Change Fuels Bogey-Free 63 — Best Round of His 2026 Season
stories.angle: Koepka's putting has been his Achilles heel this year. One putter change — one round — and he looks like a completely different golfer. He is within striking distance of a win.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
New putter. Zero bogeys. Eight under.

Brooks Koepka just had the round of his 2026 season — a 63 at the Byron Nelson.

He's one back. He's locked in. Don't be surprised by what happens next. #GolfFanrecap #Koepka #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Koepka's putter changes: he made FOUR putts outside 10 feet in Round 1.

A 17-footer for birdie. A 13-footer for eagle. Bogey-free 63.

That's not luck. That's a man who found his stroke. #GolfFanrecap #Koepka

facebook.long_form:
There is something almost theatrical about how a single piece of equipment can change the narrative of a golfer's season. Brooks Koepka has had his struggles in 2026. His ball-striking has remained formidable. His putting has not been at the level that made him one of the most clutch performers in major championship history.

So he made a change. A new putter arrived in his bag for Round 1 of the CJ Cup Byron Nelson.

The result: a bogey-free 8-under 63 — the lowest round of his 2026 PGA Tour season.

It wasn't just the numbers. It was how the birdies and the eagle came. Koepka holed a 17-foot birdie on his second hole of the day. He made a 13-foot eagle putt that gave him a run of momentum he never relinquished. He converted four putts outside 10 feet across 18 holes — the kind of day you can only have when every stroke feels completely right.

He stands one shot behind Taylor Moore heading into Round 2. He's grouped with Scheffler and Si Woo Kim for the afternoon wave. He is, once again, very much in the conversation.

For Brooks Koepka, being in a conversation like this is where he lives.
facebook.image_caption:
New putter. Zero bogeys. 8-under 63 at the Byron Nelson.

Koepka's best round of 2026 — and he's one back of the lead.

Don't look away. #GolfFanrecap #Koepka #PGATour

article.headline: New Putter, Zero Bogeys: Koepka's 63 Is the Best Round of His 2026 Season

stories.title: Aaron Rai Aftermath — Nicklaus and Faldo Praise Historic PGA Championship Win
stories.angle: The praise from Nicklaus and Faldo — two of the greatest champions in the game's history — places Rai's win in its proper historical context. His 290-1 odds victory is being described as one of the most improbable major wins of the modern era.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Jack Nicklaus and Nick Faldo praised Aaron Rai after his PGA Championship win.

When the two greatest champions in history acknowledge what you did —

That's a different kind of validation. 290-1. Historic. #GolfFanrecap #AaronRai

facebook.long_form:
More than a week after Aaron Rai's stunning 9-under PGA Championship victory at Aronimink Golf Club, the reaction from the broader golf world continues to build. And the most significant voices to weigh in may be the most significant voices in the sport's history.

Jack Nicklaus — winner of 18 major championships, the most of any player ever — praised Rai's composure and performance in the days after the victory. Nick Faldo — six-time major champion and widely considered one of the greatest ball-strikers of his era — added his own words of admiration.

That kind of recognition places Rai's win in a specific historical lineage. When the two men whose names define major championship excellence acknowledge what you achieved, it carries a weight that no highlight package or statistic can match.

What Rai did was extraordinary by any measure. He was ranked 44th in the world entering the week. He won at 290-to-1 odds — the definition of an upset. He was the first Englishman to win the PGA Championship since Jim Barnes won the first two editions of the tournament in 1916 and 1919. He was the first player of Indian heritage to win a men's major championship.

The 70-foot birdie putt at the 17th hole that sealed it. The eagle at the 9th. The final-round 65. All of it happening at Aronimink, in front of a crowd that had arrived expecting someone else to win.

Nicklaus and Faldo noticed. Golf noticed. The reverberations of what Aaron Rai achieved on May 17 will echo for years.
facebook.image_caption:
Jack Nicklaus and Nick Faldo praised Aaron Rai this week.

When the sport's greatest champions acknowledge what you did — that's a different kind of validation.

First Englishman to win PGA Championship since 1919. 290-1. Historic. #GolfFanrecap #AaronRai

article.headline: Jack Nicklaus and Nick Faldo Praise Aaron Rai After Historic PGA Championship Victory

stories.title: Nelly Korda's 2026 LPGA Dominance — 3 Wins in 6 Starts, Targeting US Women's Open
stories.angle: Korda's stat line is historically unusual: 7.97% bogey-or-worse rate (more than 4 percentage points better than anyone else on tour); only Annika Sorenstam in 2001 started a season with six consecutive top-2 finishes. She is the overwhelming pre-tournament favorite for the US Women's Open.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda in 2026:

6 starts. 3 wins. 3 runner-ups. World No. 1. Chevron Championship major.

Only Annika Sorenstam (2001) started a season with 6 straight top-2 finishes.

US Women's Open is June 4-7. Be afraid. #GolfFanrecap #NellyKorda #LPGA

facebook.long_form:
There is a comparison being made in women's golf right now that you do not make lightly. Analysts are invoking Annika Sorenstam's 2001 season — arguably the most dominant performance by any player in the history of the LPGA Tour.

And it applies to Nelly Korda in 2026.

In six starts this season, Korda has won three times and finished second three times. Six starts. Zero finishes outside the top two. The only player who achieved six consecutive top-2 finishes to begin a season was Sorenstam in 2001.

Her wins include the Chevron Championship — her third career major, which returned her to the world No. 1 ranking — and two subsequent victories, including her win at the Riviera Maya Open at Mayakoba by four strokes. She became the first player since Lorena Ochoa in 2008 to win an LPGA tournament by four strokes or more in back-to-back weeks.

The statistical picture is similarly remarkable. Her bogey-or-worse rate of 7.97% is not just the lowest on the LPGA Tour — it's more than four full percentage points better than anyone else in the field. She is getting up-and-down at a 75.0% clip, also the best on tour.

At 27 years old, she became the youngest American to reach 18 career LPGA victories in 46 years — since Nancy Lopez did it in 1980.

The US Women's Open is scheduled for June 4-7 at Riviera Country Club in Pacific Palisades, California. Korda arrives as the overwhelming favorite and the form player of the decade. The only question is whether anyone in the world can disrupt what she's doing right now.
facebook.image_caption:
3 wins. 3 runner-ups. 6 starts. World No. 1. Major champion.

Nelly Korda is having an Annika Sorenstam-level 2026 season — and the US Women's Open is coming June 4-7.

Be afraid. Be very afraid. #GolfFanrecap #NellyKorda #LPGA

article.headline: Nelly Korda's 2026 LPGA Season Is Historically Dominant — and the US Women's Open Is Next

stories.title: LIV Golf Korea Preview — Tour Heads to Busan After Herbert's Virginia Wire-to-Wire Win
stories.angle: Herbert's Virginia win was one of the most dominant performances in LIV Golf 2026 — wire-to-wire, four shots clear at -24. Korea represents a new test as the tour moves to Asia for the first time this season.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf heads to Busan, Korea. May 28-31.

Lucas Herbert leads the individual standings after his wire-to-wire Virginia win at -24.

First Asian stop of the 2026 LIV season. 57 players. No cut. 72 holes. #GolfFanrecap #LIVGolf

facebook.long_form:
Lucas Herbert delivered the most dominant wire-to-wire performance on the LIV Golf circuit this season when he won at Trump National Golf Club in Virginia, finishing at 24-under par and winning by four shots over Sergio Garcia. It was his first LIV individual trophy. He leads the 2026 individual standings with 99.18 points.

Now the tour moves to Asia.

LIV Golf Korea takes place from May 28-31 at Asiad Country Club in Busan, South Korea — the first Asian stop of the 2026 season. The full 57-player field competes over 72 holes with no cut, meaning every player in the field will finish all four rounds.

The team standings heading into Korea have Ripper GC at the top, followed by 4Aces GC — which won the team title at Virginia in a playoff — and Legion XIII in third. The individual and team races will both be alive throughout the Korea event.

The move to Busan brings the tour to a market with deep interest in golf and significant commercial partnerships. Korea has produced some of the most decorated players in professional golf history, and the connection between the tour and its Korean audience is meaningful.

For the LIV field, Korea represents a new challenge in a new environment — always an opportunity for someone to separate themselves from the pack.
facebook.image_caption:
LIV Golf is heading to Busan, Korea. May 28-31.

Herbert leads individual standings after Virginia. Ripper GC on top in teams.

First Asian stop of 2026 — who takes the next title? #GolfFanrecap #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Golf Heads to Korea: Herbert Leads Standings After Wire-to-Wire Virginia Victory
